title: Umami Flaky Adventurous Spanish Chia Seed Crumble
description: This innovative chia seed crumble combines umami flavors with a spicy kick and is nut-free-friendly, perfect for adding adventurous flair to breakfast or dessert dishes.

ingredients.name: 1/2 teaspoon ground cayenne pepper (hot chilli powder)
1 tablespoon apple cider vinegar
ingredients.notes: Helps in fermenting process if using active dry yeast instead of ferment

ingredients.name: 1/4 cup unsweetened almond milk (or any dairy-free alternative)
ingredients.notes: Soaks the seeds to activate their natural gel-forming properties

ingredients.name: 2 tablespoons maple syrup
ingredients.notes: For sweetness and depth of flavor

instructions: Soak the chia seeds in almond milk until they form a gel, about 5 minutes. If using active dry yeast for fermenting instead, mix with umami mushrooms, cayenne pepper, and apple cider vinegar; let sit overnight covered.
In a small bowl, combine maple syrup, salt, and soaked chia seeds. Mix well to create an even consistency.
Preheat your oven to 350°F (175°C). Line a baking sheet with parchment paper.
Press the mixture into a thin layer on the prepared pan. Bake for about 20-25 minutes or until golden brown and crispy.
Let it cool slightly before serving as a delicious dessert crumble.
